Petition To Contest And Set Aside Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Continued To Dec-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To: 1) Disqualify Laura O Kane 2) Order A Pre-Judgement Lien 3) Order An Accounting
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Continued To Dec-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299927 The Roger W. Sleight 2010 and
PTR-19-303227 The Roger W. Sleight.

Appearances: James Finsten appeared by zoom for and with Marilyn Bessey (Co-Trustee) by zoom.
Laura O'Kane and Fey Epling appeared by zoom for and with Mark Backer (Co-Trustee) by zoom.
Joshua Sleight appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Zachary Sleight appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Sharon Sleight appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

All parties indicated that they received the Court's order of November 9, 2020. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to the issue of whether or not San Francisco is the proper venue. Joshua Sleight stated that they are looking into getting an Attorney, but they are having trouble finding someone. The Court noted that the substitution of attorney filed in case number PTR-16-299927 is sufficient for both cases as the matters have been consolidated. All matters are continued to December 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. All parties are to look at the issues raised in the November 9, 2020 order and are to be prepared to address them.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlan, CSR #8875, 415-834-1114, ma@scanlanstone.com.

Petition To: 1) Disqualify Laura O Kane 2) Order A Pre-Judgement Lien 3) Order An Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-28-2020 Continued To Dec-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299927 The Roger W. Sleight 2010 Trust and
PTR-19-303227 The Roger W. sleight Trust.

Appearances; Mark Malachowski for Zachary Sleight, Sharon Sleight and Joshua Sleight (Petitioners/Objectors).
James Finsten appeared by zoom for and with Marilyn Bessey (Co-Trustee) by zoom.
Laura O'Kane and Fey Epling appeared by zoom for Mark Backer (Co-Trustee/Respondent).

The Court informed the Parties that this was put on his calendar in error, that there are several motions on Judge Quinn's calendar and that the Probate Examiner has raised the issue of appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL"). Mr. Malachowski stated that he was hoping to get some rulings today. The Court stated that it is not prepared to issue any rulings today and asked if anyone is objecting to Ms. O'Kane filing a petition for appointment of a Guardian Ad Litem. Mr. Finsten stated he agrees there will be a mediation and some adjustment to the trust, that they were not aware of any Minors and that Ms. O'Kane has requested that they not contact any member of the Backer family. Ms. O'Kane stated that appointment of a GAL is appropriate, that the fees for the GAL will affect the trust estate and that she was told by her Client that the family felt they were being harassed by Ms. Bessey. Mr. Malachowski and Mr. Finsten indicated that they do not oppose/object to appointment of a GAL. Ms. O'Kane stated that Mr. Malachowski filed a motion to be relieved as Counsel indicating that there has been no communication and a complete breakdown in the Attorney Client relationship, that it is on calendar for February 9th, that there is a contentious of the peculiar relationship with Ms. Bessey and asked if the Court on its own motion could advance the hearing. Mr. Malachowski stated that the Sleights are in pro per in the civil case, that they filed a dismissal with prejudice, that he is asking to be removed in the probate case, that this is the date the Clerks came back with and that the Clients are seeking other Counsel. The Court stated that it will not advance the hearing, that there is a chance that a substitution of Counsel may be coming and that there is an ex parte procedure to advance hearing dates. Discussion was also held regarding the dismissal only having one Party's signature. Mr. Malachowski stated that he has signatures from all three of the Clients, that he will mail the documents to Counsel and that he needs to have a Judge tell him if he has to sit for depositions, or not. The Petition to Contest and Set Aside Purported Trust, etc., the Petition to Disqualify Laura O'Kane, etc., the First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc., the Report and First and Final Account of Mark Backer, etc., and the Petition for Instructions Regarding Invasion of Principal, etc., are continued to December 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Reporter: Mary Bardellini, CSR #2976, 530-320-2222, bardellini@sbcglobal.net

Petition To Contest And Set Aside Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust

Petition To Contest And Set Aside Purported Trust And To Impose Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Jun-22-2020 Continued To Jul-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To: 1) Disqualify Laura O Kane 2) Order A Pre-Judgement Lien 3) Order An Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-22-2020 Continued To Jul-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Finsten appeared by telephone for and with Marilyn Bessie (by telephone).
Laura O'Kane appeared by telephone for Mark Backer (Respondent/Co-Trustee).
Fey Epling for Mark Backer (Respondent/Co-Trustee).
Mark Malachowski appeared by telephone for Zachary Sleight, Sharon Sleight and Joshua Sleight.

The Court noted that there are several petitions (5), that the civil action is being litigated, that there is a demurrer, reminds the Parties of the cost of litigation and questions if the Parties have made any decision regarding mediation. Mr. Malachowski stated that he is happy to go to mediation, that he is looking for an accounting, that they have not received information and that his Accountant seems to think that there is three to four million dollars that are not accounted for. Ms. O'Kane stated that they originally sent two draft accountings from the time Roger was declared incapacitated, that she explained that they would file Mr. Backer's accounting first, that Ms. Bessey also filed an accounting for her time and that there was an issue of not having original bank statements. Ms. O'Kane also raised the issue of Mr. Malachowski being able to request any documents he wants through discovery or by issuing a third-party subpoena. The Court raised the issue of the expense to the trust and suggest stay of the civil action until the probate issues are unfolded. Mr. Malachowski stated that Roger was never ruled incapacitated, that he would be happy to go to mediation, but he needs the bank statements and the credit card statements, and that he will suggest to his Client to stay the civil action. Mr. Finsten stated that he thinks Ms. Bessey is amenable to resolution and that there is a massive amount of discovery that has already been served. Ms. Epling raised the issue of the Court consolidating the probate matters, or all three of the matters stating that the issues are overlapping and that she will speak with her Client about mediation. Ms. Epling also stated that the issue is that the petition seeks to undo the estate planning, spoke of the terms of the trusts that were drafted and provided history. The Court on its own motion will consolidate PTR-19-303227 with PTR-16-299927. PTR-16-299927 will be the lead case. The Court informed the Parties that the order will indicate that everything is to be filed in case number PTR-16-299927 (lead case). The Court also stated that any duplicative discovery in every petition needs only to be answered once and that the Parties should discuss any informal information that can be exchanged. The Court informed the Parties that they do not qualify for the probate pro bono mediation, spoke of the Court's Judicial Mediators and suggested that the Parties adapt to some flexibility of the discovery rules. Ms. O'Kane stated that it is premature to enter into mediation and that she would like to see discovery responded to. The Petition to Disqualify Laura O'Kane, etc., and the Petition to Contest and Set Aside Purported Trust, etc. are continued to July 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see where the Parties are, to set the petitions for hearing and for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
